purpose: A high-signal conversation library for Silicon Valley circles.

items.title: The Bitter Lesson
items.summary: A short, famous argument that general methods using computation tend to beat hand-built knowledge over time.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It instantly opens the scale-versus-craft debate that dominates AI rooms.

items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.summary: The paper that introduced the Transformer architecture and made modern LLMs possible.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It is the technical before-and-after line for the generative AI era.

items.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
items.summary: OpenAI’s flagship disclosure on GPT-4’s multimodality, benchmark performance, and limitations.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It bridges model capability, product strategy, and the practical reality of frontier AI.
items.what_kind_of_people_would_discuss_it: Founders, AI engineers, product teams, investors

items.title: Software Is Changing (Again)
items.summary: Karpathy reframes software as shifting again under AI, with English, prompts, and agents becoming part of the programming stack.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It is one of the clearest ways to discuss AI-native software without falling into hype.

items.title: 2026 AI Index Report
items.summary: The most current Stanford AI Index reports AI investment, capability progress, adoption, and governance gaps.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It gives people a shared factual baseline instead of vibes.

items.title: Startup = Growth
items.summary: One of Graham’s clearest statements that small differences in startup growth rate compound into radically different outcomes.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: Founders and investors constantly argue about growth quality, and this essay gives them a shared language.

items.title: Do Things that Don’t Scale
items.summary: A canonical YC-era argument for doing manual, unscalable work to get the first users and learn what matters.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: Almost every founder can tell a 'we did the ugly thing early' story.

items.title: Before the Startup
items.summary: Talks about how founders think before they start, how ideas emerge, and why judgment matters more than planning.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: A natural entry point for anyone deciding whether to start a company.

items.title: Founder Mode
items.summary: Argues that founders should sometimes manage companies differently from professional managers as they scale.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: It touches a live Silicon Valley argument about founder autonomy, management, and culture.

items.title: How to Get Startup Ideas
items.summary: Says good startup ideas are usually found by observing real problems rather than brainstorming them abstractly.
items.why_it_is_a_good_conversation_topic: Idea quality and problem selection are evergreen founder-room arguments.

safe_to_bring_up_at_dinner_or_networking: the classic Silicon Valley origin story
concise_synthesis: Silicon Valley conversation keeps circling a few durable questions: does scale beat theory, do founders need special operating freedom, how do network effects and distribution create moats, and how do funds, LPs, and regulation shape what gets built?
gaps: global VC history outside the U.S.
biotech, climate, defense, and hardware VC sub-histories
term sheets, legal infrastructure, and secondary mechanics
